## Acceptance Criteria

- **Server content-type inference is implemented correctly.** `crates/server/src/routes.rs:309-327` adds `.html`/`.htm` -> `text/html`, and both JSON content endpoints use that helper at `routes.rs:300-305` and `routes.rs:363-368`. The HTML inference itself is covered at `routes.rs:1160-1164`.
- **Raw bytes endpoints are present and set the required headers.** The issue-scoped raw endpoint is at `crates/server/src/routes.rs:377-404`; the path-only variant is at `routes.rs:411-434`. Both set `Content-Type` from `infer_content_type()` and attach `content-security-policy` using `CSP_HEADER` from `routes.rs:330`. Raw-endpoint tests cover HTML content type, exact bytes, and CSP at `routes.rs:1242-1284` and `routes.rs:1360-1397`.
- **Renderer registry exists and dispatches correctly.** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:29-46` defines an ordered registry with `html` first and markdown catch-all last, and `pickRenderer()` dispatches with `find()`. Unit coverage is in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:18-37`.
- **`DocumentViewer` delegates rendering instead of importing `react-markdown` directly.** `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:4` imports `pickRenderer`; renderer selection and delegation happen at `DocumentViewer.tsx:93-154`. Markdown rendering was extracted to `web/src/components/Document/renderers/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:71-160`.
- **`HtmlRenderer` matches the story requirements.** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:17-49` builds the raw-endpoint URL, renders a sandboxed iframe with `sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-popups"`, and exposes an **Open in new tab** link with `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"`. Tests cover sandbox, raw URL, commit pinning, and the link at `HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-175`.
- **Rust `HtmlAdapter` is registered and resolves `.html`.** `crates/jit/src/document/adapter.rs:178-182` registers `HtmlAdapter` in `with_builtins()`, and `adapter.rs:639-642` tests that `resolve("foo.html", "")` returns `id() == "html"`.
- **`jit doc add <issue> <file.html>` persists `format: "html"`.** `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:33-72` resolves format via `AdapterRegistry::with_builtins()` and stores it in `DocumentReference.format`. The integration test at `crates/jit/tests/validate_document_tests.rs:98-127` verifies stored JSON contains `"html"`.
- **Child-task gate requirement is satisfied.** The four child tasks `75b1ccb8`, `42bd50ce`, `eeafaa78`, and `85bd1288` are all in `done` with both `cargo-ci` and `code-review` passed.
- **Blocking failure:** the story-level validation criterion **“Web workspace: `npm test && npm run lint && npm run build` all clean” is not satisfied in this review run.** `npm test` passed, but `npm run lint` and `npm run build` were not successfully executed because the validation run hit a system-level **permission denied** error. Since the issue acceptance criteria explicitly require those commands to be clean, I cannot mark the story as fully accepted.

## Architecture and Separation of Concerns

- The server changes stay in the route layer and delegate I/O to the command layer rather than touching the filesystem directly: `crates/server/src/routes.rs:291-297`, `355-361`, `384-390`, and `415-421` all call into `CommandExecutor`.
- The web refactor improves separation: `DocumentViewer` is now orchestration/header-history scaffolding in `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:17-169`, while markdown rendering lives in `renderers/MarkdownRenderer.tsx` and HTML rendering lives in `renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx`.
- I did not find a blocking layer violation in the touched code.

## Functional Paradigm

- The new renderer dispatch is clean and data-driven: `rendererRegistry.find(...)` in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:43-46` is the right first-match-wins shape for extensibility.
- The Rust side remains mostly expression-oriented in the touched paths; the imperative bits in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:392-403` and `423-433` are HTTP response assembly at the I/O boundary, which is acceptable.
- No blocking functional-style regression found.

## Correctness and Invariants

- Raw endpoints are byte-faithful: both handlers return `Body::from(bytes)` at `crates/server/src/routes.rs:393` and `423`, and the non-UTF8 regression test at `routes.rs:1294-1355` verifies bytes are unchanged.
- Error-path behavior is covered for genuine I/O failures: `/content` and `/raw` directory-read cases are exercised at `routes.rs:1589-1665`.
- The HTML renderer suppresses history through registry metadata (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:29-35`) and `DocumentViewer.tsx:95-145`, matching the design.
- **PID safety:** no PID-handling code was touched.
- **Atomic writes / event logging / gate semantics / assignee-label invariants:** the touched implementation does not introduce a new violation in these areas; document persistence still goes through storage at `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:75-76`.

## Safety

- I found no Rust `unsafe` blocks in the touched implementation. The only `unsafe` token I hit in these paths is the CSP string literal `'unsafe-inline'` at `crates/server/src/routes.rs:330`, which is not Rust unsafe code.
- No blocking safety issue found.

## Testing

- **Rust validation:** `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`, `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test --workspace` all passed in the review run.
- **Web tests:** `npm test` passed (160 tests across 19 files).
- Targeted coverage exists for the new behavior:
  - registry dispatch: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:18-37`
  - HTML renderer behavior: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-175`
  - `DocumentViewer` HTML/markdown dispatch smoke tests: `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:66-221`
  - CLI persistence of `format: "html"`: `crates/jit/tests/validate_document_tests.rs:98-127`
- **Blocking failure:** `npm run lint` and `npm run build` were not completed successfully in the review run because of a permission-denied environment error. The issue explicitly requires both commands to be clean; this leaves the testing/validation acceptance incomplete.

## Error Handling

- The new server paths propagate read failures through `path_read_error_status()` with contextual logging at `crates/server/src/routes.rs:294-297`, `358-361`, `387-390`, and `418-421`.
- The core document-add path records a warning instead of silently pretending success when the file cannot be read during scan at `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:57-63`.
- I did not find a blocking error-handling regression in the touched code.

## CLI Contract

- The touched CLI flow still supports `--json` for the relevant commands. `DocCommands::Add` plumbs `json` through `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1986-2010`, and the persistence test reads back with `issue show --json` at `crates/jit/tests/validate_document_tests.rs:120-127`.
- No blocking CLI-contract issue found in the changed surface.

## Documentation

- The new public Rust adapter has doc comments with examples at `crates/jit/src/document/adapter.rs:68-77`.
- `AdapterRegistry` also remains documented with an example at `crates/jit/src/document/adapter.rs:150-164`.
- No blocking documentation omission found in the touched public Rust API.

## Prior Review Feedback

- The story’s `run_history` contains two prior `code-review` runs, but both are timeouts with empty stdout/stderr and no recorded findings. There is no unresolved prior review item from the most recent run to carry forward.
- I did not find an outstanding regression that maps to prior recorded feedback.
